TextBlock.Text: Soviet assault takes NATO by surprise

StackPanel.TextBlock:
FRANKFURT / TOKYO — Yesterday at 9:00am GMT, Soviet and Warsaw Pact forces launched a massive global offensive, driving west into Europe and hitting U.S. and Japanese positions in the Pacific.
In Europe, Soviet armored divisions pushed through the Fulda Gap into West Germany and threatening NATO’s defensive strongholds. NATO forces, while mobilizing reinforcements, are engaged in intense combat across multiple fronts, with heavy casualties reported. U.S. Army units are bearing the brunt of the fighting.
In the Pacific, Soviet air and naval forces have attacked U.S. bases in Korea and Okinawa. These attacks were coordinated with Chinese and North Korean forces. Japan has come under intermittent bombardment by Soviet conventional ballistic missiles.
See WAR on page 2.

TextBlock.Text: U.S Army tanks move east towards the frontline on Wednesday morning. United States, British, French, and West German forces are heavily engaged along the border with East Germany. Nuclear weapons have not yet been utilized by either side, but NATO commanders are not ruling out this possibility. Evacuations of German civilians from the combat areas are underway. U.S. officials urge calm at home. (AP Wire Photo)

StackPanel.TextBlock:
NEW YORK — Stock markets around the globe have been forced to shutter after the outbreak of war in Europe and the Pacific triggered a wave of panic selling, sending financial markets into freefall. Major exchanges in New York, London, and Tokyo halted trading as indices plummeted by historic margins, wiping out trillions in market value in mere hours. Investor confidence has evaporated in the face of unprecedented geopolitical instability, with fears of prolonged conflict driving the selloff. This sudden halt in trading marks one of the most severe shocks to the global financial system in decades.
See MARKETS on page 3.

TextBlock.Text: NYSE has halted trading as of 12:00 PM EDT yesterday.
